ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

I would like to get your perceptions of the behaviors of your peers, that is youth your same age who live in your neighborhood (or go to your school, but not just your own friends or people you know well. Among 20 peers in your school or neighborhood how many of them do you think have been tested for HIV/AIDS?
Notes: 0=None or almost none (0 or 1); 5=About a quarter ( Approximately 25% or between 1-5 peers); 10=About half (Approximately 50% or between 6-10 peers); 15=About three quarters (Approximately 75% or between 11-15 peers); 20=Nearly all (Approximately 100% or 16-20 peers)

ElementDescription: Now I would like to get your perception of your close friends. Please think about your peers who you spend time, your age and sex, such as your good friends. In thinking of your THREE closest friends, how many of them do you think have been tested for HIV/AIDS?
